name: Random Powerups
description: A generated reward mechanic that grants run-scoped powerups from weighted pools after rooms, events, shops, bosses, or pickups.
design_notes: Random powerups should feel surprising without becoming arbitrary. The generator needs reward pools, rarity weights, unlock filters, duplicate handling, seed logging, and player-facing previews. Designers should separate permanent unlock availability from current run eligibility so the pool can grow safely over the lifetime of the game.
design_purpose: Create variety between runs by changing tools, stats, actions, or synergies.
Reward combat, exploration, risk rooms, shops, and events with build-shaping options.
Let designers tune build archetypes through tags, rarity, and pool membership.
player_fantasy: The player feels each reward could open a new build path or transform how the current run plays.

parameters.name: reward_pool_id
parameters.description: Candidate pool selected by room, event, or reward source.

parameters.name: rng_stream_id
parameters.description: Random stream used to draw reward candidates.

parameters.name: item_rarity_tier
parameters.description: Rarity class selected from the reward weighting profile.

parameters.name: unlock_pool_id
parameters.description: Account or character unlock set allowed for this run.

parameters.name: duplicate_reward_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule for owned or recently offered powerups.

parameters.name: powerup_instance_id
parameters.description: Run-scoped instance used for stacking and removal.

edge_cases: The reward pool is empty after unlock filters, duplicate rules, and character restrictions apply.
A granted powerup changes eligibility for other options in the same reward screen.
A save reload happens after the drop appears but before the player collects it.
Two rewards with different ids share the same stacking slot and conflict silently.
A content update removes a powerup definition used by an active run save.
common_bugs: Reward draws are not deterministic because pool iteration follows unordered dictionary keys.
Duplicate prevention checks display names instead of stable powerup ids.
Temporary powerups persist after floor transition because cleanup does not inspect powerup_instance_id.
Rarity weights are applied before eligibility filters and skew the intended distribution.
Powerup previews read base stats instead of current run modifiers and mislead the player.
balancing_notes: Reward weighting should support both build discovery and comeback potential without guaranteeing ideal synergies.
Permanent unlocks increase pool size, so older content may need weighting or tags to stay visible.
Converting invalid duplicates to currency can reduce frustration, but too much conversion makes rewards feel interchangeable.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear text for powerup effects, stack behavior, and whether the effect is temporary.
Let players review collected powerups outside fast reward moments.
Use readable icons and labels so rarity and build tags are not communicated by color alone.

implementation_notes.unity: Use ScriptableObject powerup definitions but draw rewards through a service that takes run_id, reward_pool_id, rng_stream_id, unlock_pool_id, and duplicate rules. Persist offered or dropped powerup ids before showing pickups to prevent redraws after reload.
implementation_notes.godot: Store powerup Resources in versioned registries and perform eligibility filtering before weighted selection. Commit powerup_instance_id into run state, then emit signals for stat recompute, UI list updates, and pickup presentation.
implementation_notes.unreal: Data Assets can define powerup tags, rarity tiers, and stacking policies. Server-authoritative runs should draw rewards on the server, replicate offered ids, and apply granted instances through a deterministic modifier component.
implementation_notes.web: Keep reward pools as sorted JSON arrays and use seeded RNG for candidate draws. Store offer records and granted powerup_instance_id values in local or server run state so browser refreshes do not reroll rewards.

relationships.reason: Random powerups become more strategic when players choose between generated offers instead of receiving one blindly.
Powerup selection needs reward pools, rarity weights, duplicate rules, and deterministic grant records.
Powerup pools gain depth when relic effects create meaningful build synergies and tradeoffs.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses random items to create different run builds.

example_games.title: Risk of Rain 2
example_games.note: Uses item drops and stacking effects as core run growth.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses randomized card, relic, and reward offers to shape each run.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses randomized weapons, skills, and rewards to shape each run.
